---
title: Expectation over the posterior distribution of predicted risks provides partial protection agaisnt overfitting
author: "Mohsen Sadatsafavi"
date: "2026.05.27"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
  word_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(ggrepel)
library(brglm2)
library(brms)
library(glmnet)
library(mcmapper)
library(parallel)
library(foreach)
library(doSNOW)
library(doRNG)
library(WeightedROC)
library(pROC)
library(predtools)
library(tableone)
library(randtoolbox)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggh4x)  # facet_grid2 with independent y-scales per panel

res_dir <- "M:/Projects/2026/Project.BayesCPM/results/wip/"


source("core.R")
```





## Simulation studies
```{r}

# =========================================================================
# Settings
# =========================================================================
settings <- list(
  # Design factors
  M                = 275,
  true_models      = c(3, 10, 30),
  candidate_models = c(3, 10, 30),
  prevalences      = c(0.05, 0.15, 0.30),
  epvs             = c(5, 10, 30),
  correlations     = c(0, 0.25, 0.50),
  # Use quasi-Monte Carlo (Sobol) for population X to reduce MC error on
  # population-level metrics. Training sample X_sam always uses plain MC.
  use_qmc          = TRUE,
  n_pop            = 1e4,
  design_seed      = 42,

  # log-F(m, m) prior strength for slopes (intercept stays flat).
  # m = 1 is Greenland & Mansournia's preferred general-purpose default.
  logf_m = 2,

  # Methods to compare
   methods = c('pe_f', 
              'pm_f', 'pm_f_mk', 'pm_f_pp', '_pm_f_cpp',
              'pe_j',  'pm_j', 'pm_j_mk', 'pm_j_pp', '_pm_j_cpp', 
              'pe_lf', 'pm_lf', 'pm_lf_mk', 'pm_lf_pp',  '_pm_lf_cpp',
              'pe_br', 'pm_br', 'pm_br_mk', 'pm_br_pp', '_pm_br_cpp',
              'pe_lasso_min', 'pe_ridge_min'),

  # Performance metrics
  # NOTE: metrics are now evaluated against pi_true_pop (soft labels)
  # rather than y_pop. This removes Bernoulli sampling noise.
  metrics = c("dkl", "mse", "cstat", "oe_ratio", "cal_int", "cal_slp", "snb"),
  # NB thresholds as quantiles of true risk:
  z_quant = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75),   #c(0.5, 1, 1.5),

  # Credible-interval coverage levels to evaluate. For each level, the
  # eta-scale credible interval is constructed from the multivariate-normal
  # posterior approximation and coverage is measured against the known
  # population true linear predictor. Applies only to the four Bayesian
  # families (flat, Jeffreys, log-F, Bayesian ridge) — frequentist CV-tuned
  # ridge / LASSO have no Laplace posterior so coverage is not defined.
  # Set to NULL or empty to skip.
  cri_cov = c(0.95),

  # Posterior-distribution calibration via the probability integral
  # transform (PIT). For each individual, u_i = Phi((eta_pseudo_i - mu_i)
  # / sigma_i). If the Laplace-approximated posterior is calibrated, the
  # u_i are uniformly distributed on [0,1]. We summarise this via three
  # quantile checks: F_emp(0.10), F_emp(0.50), F_emp(0.90) — fractions of
  # PIT values below each fixed threshold. Targets are 0.10, 0.50, and
  # 0.90 respectively. The pattern across the three is interpretable:
  # both 10% and 90% above target = posterior too narrow; below target =
  # too wide; both shifted in the same direction = location bias.
  # Computed automatically whenever cri_cov is non-empty.

  # Execution
  n_cores    = NULL,
  chunk_size = 100
)

saveRDS(settings,  file = paste0(res_dir, "settings_synth.rds"))


```




```{R}
# =========================================================================
# generate_sim_design
# =========================================================================
generate_sim_design <- function(settings) {
  set.seed(settings$design_seed)

  fixed <- expand.grid(
    true_p      = settings$true_models,
    candidate_p = settings$candidate_models,
    prevalence  = settings$prevalences,
    epv         = settings$epvs,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  n_scenarios <- nrow(fixed)
  M <- settings$M

  fixed$n_sample <- with(fixed, ceiling((candidate_p + 1) * epv / prevalence))

  design <- fixed[rep(seq_len(n_scenarios), each = M), ]
  design$iteration <- rep(seq_len(M), times = n_scenarios)
  design$scenario  <- rep(seq_len(n_scenarios), each = M)

  n_total <- nrow(design)

  design$n_binary <- sapply(design$true_p, function(p) sample(0:p, 1))
  design$correlation <- sample(settings$correlations, n_total, replace = TRUE)

  design$n_continuous    <- design$true_p - design$n_binary
  design$misspecified    <- design$true_p != design$candidate_p
  design$candidate_larger  <- design$candidate_p > design$true_p
  design$candidate_smaller <- design$candidate_p < design$true_p

  design <- design[, c(
    "scenario", "iteration",
    "true_p", "candidate_p", "misspecified",
    "candidate_larger", "candidate_smaller",
    "prevalence", "epv", "n_sample",
    "n_binary", "n_continuous", "correlation"
  )]

  rownames(design) <- NULL

  cat("Simulation design summary:\n")
  cat("  Fixed scenarios:", n_scenarios, "\n")
  cat("  Replicates per scenario:", M, "\n")
  cat("  Total rows:", n_total, "\n")
  cat("  Sample size range:", min(design$n_sample), "-", max(design$n_sample), "\n")
  cat("\n  Scenario breakdown:\n")
  cat("    True model sizes:", paste(settings$true_models, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("    Candidate model sizes:", paste(settings$candidate_models, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("    Prevalences:", paste(settings$prevalences, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("    EPVs:", paste(settings$epvs, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("    Correctly specified:", sum(!design$misspecified), "/", n_total,
      "(", round(100 * mean(!design$misspecified), 1), "%)\n")
  cat("    Candidate larger:", sum(design$candidate_larger), "/", n_total,
      "(", round(100 * mean(design$candidate_larger), 1), "%)\n")
  cat("    Candidate smaller:", sum(design$candidate_smaller), "/", n_total,
      "(", round(100 * mean(design$candidate_smaller), 1), "%)\n")

  design
}

# =========================================================================
# generate_from_design
# =========================================================================
generate_from_design <- function(design, row_idx, settings) {
  r <- design[row_idx, ]

  n       <- r$n_sample
  p_true  <- r$true_p
  p_cand  <- r$candidate_p
  n_bin   <- r$n_binary
  cor_val <- r$correlation
  prev    <- r$prevalence
  n_pop   <- settings$n_pop

  # Plain MC: standard Gaussian draw then Cholesky rotation
  generate_X <- function(n_obs, p_true, n_bin, L) {
    Z <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs * p_true), n_obs, p_true)
    X <- Z %*% L
    if (n_bin > 0) {
      for (j in 1:n_bin) {
        X[, j] <- as.numeric(X[, j] > 0)
      }
    }
    colnames(X) <- paste0("X", seq_len(p_true))
    X
  }

  # QMC: unscrambled Sobol, qnorm to standard normal, Cholesky rotation.
  # init = TRUE restarts the sequence each call so population X is
  # deterministic given (p_true, n_bin, L) for reproducibility. Clamp
  # to avoid qnorm returning Inf at sequence endpoints.
  generate_X_qmc <- function(n_obs, p_true, n_bin, L) {
    sob <- randtoolbox::sobol(n_obs, dim = p_true, init = TRUE)
    sob <- pmin(pmax(sob, 1e-10), 1 - 1e-10)
    Z <- qnorm(sob)
    X <- Z %*% L
    if (n_bin > 0) {
      for (j in 1:n_bin) {
        X[, j] <- as.numeric(X[, j] > 0)
      }
    }
    colnames(X) <- paste0("X", seq_len(p_true))
    X
  }

  build_df <- function(X, y, p_true, p_cand, n_obs) {
    if (p_cand > p_true) {
      n_extra <- p_cand - p_true
      X_extra <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs * n_extra), n_obs, n_extra)
      colnames(X_extra) <- paste0("X", (p_true + 1):p_cand)
      X_all <- cbind(X, X_extra)
    } else {
      X_all <- X
    }
    df <- as.data.frame(X_all)
    df$Y <- y
    df
  }

  Sigma_x <- matrix(cor_val, p_true, p_true)
  diag(Sigma_x) <- 1
  L <- chol(Sigma_x)

  set.seed(r$scenario * 10000 + r$iteration)
  true_beta <-  rexp(p_true, rate=1/0.354)*sample(c(-1, 1), p_true, replace = TRUE) #rnorm(p_true, mean = 0, sd = 0.5) #rt(p_true,5)/sqrt(5/3)/2 #rexp(p_true, rate = 1/0.354) * sample(c(-1, 1), p_true, replace = TRUE) #rnorm(p_true, mean = 0, sd = 0.5)

  # Population X: Sobol if enabled, else plain MC.
  # Training X (X_sam) is always plain MC — methods must be evaluated on
  # genuine random samples, not low-discrepancy designs.
  if (isTRUE(settings$use_qmc)) {
    X_pop <- generate_X_qmc(n_pop, p_true, n_bin, L)
  } else {
    X_pop <- generate_X(n_pop, p_true, n_bin, L)
  }

  find_intercept <- function(X, beta, target_prev, tol = 1e-6) {
    lo <- -10; hi <- 10
    for (i in 1:100) {
      mid <- (lo + hi) / 2
      p_mean <- mean(plogis(mid + X %*% beta))
      if (p_mean < target_prev) lo <- mid else hi <- mid
      if (abs(p_mean - target_prev) < tol) break
    }
    mid
  }

  intercept <- find_intercept(X_pop, true_beta, prev)
  true_vars <- c(intercept, true_beta)
  names(true_vars) <- c("Intercept", paste0("X", seq_len(p_true)))

  eta_pop <- as.numeric(intercept + X_pop %*% true_beta)
  pi_pop <- plogis(eta_pop)
  y_pop <- rbinom(n_pop, 1, pi_pop)
  df_pop <- build_df(X_pop, y_pop, p_true, p_cand, n_pop)

  X_sam <- generate_X(n, p_true, n_bin, L)
  eta_sam <- as.numeric(intercept + X_sam %*% true_beta)
  pi_sam <- plogis(eta_sam)
  y_sam <- rbinom(n, 1, pi_sam)
  df_sam <- build_df(X_sam, y_sam, p_true, p_cand, n)

  true_names <- paste0("X", seq_len(p_true))
  cand_names <- paste0("X", seq_len(p_cand))

  formula_true <- as.formula(paste("Y ~", paste(true_names, collapse = " + ")))
  formula_cand <- as.formula(paste("Y ~", paste(cand_names, collapse = " + ")))

  # Pseudo-true linear predictor for the candidate model.
  #
  # Three cases by predictor count (predictors are X1..Xp_true and X1..Xp_cand
  # in this design, so the count comparison is sufficient):
  #
  #   1. p_cand == p_true: candidate exactly matches truth.
  #      pseudo-true beta = true beta, so eta_pseudo_pop = eta_pop.
  #   2. p_cand > p_true:  candidate nests truth with redundant extras.
  #      pseudo-true beta = (true beta, 0, ..., 0), reproducing the truth
  #      exactly in the candidate's coordinate system. Coverage target is
  #      still eta_pop — no regression needed.
  #   3. p_cand < p_true:  candidate misses some true predictors.
  #      pseudo-true beta is the KL projection: a population logistic
  #      regression of pi_pop (soft labels) on the candidate predictors.
  #      This is the only case where a regression is actually needed.
  if (p_cand >= p_true) {
    eta_pseudo_pop <- eta_pop
  } else {
    # Build the candidate's population design matrix locally (mm_pop in
    # sim() is built from df_pop and formula_cand; same construction here).
    mm_pop_cand <- model.matrix(formula_cand, df_pop)
    pseudo_fit <- suppressWarnings(
      glm.fit(mm_pop_cand, pi_pop, family = binomial())
    )
    eta_pseudo_pop <- as.numeric(mm_pop_cand %*% pseudo_fit$coefficients)
  }

  list(
    sample         = df_sam,
    population     = df_pop,
    pi_true_sample = pi_sam,
    pi_true_pop    = pi_pop,
    eta_pseudo_pop = eta_pseudo_pop,
    true_vars      = true_vars,
    true_beta      = true_beta,
    formula_true   = formula_true,
    formula_cand   = formula_cand,
    design_row     = r
  )
}



# =========================================================================
# sim
# =========================================================================
sim <- function(i_row) {
  sim_parms <- generate_from_design(df_design, i_row, settings)

  df_samp <- sim_parms$sample
  df_pop  <- sim_parms$population

  fm_model <- sim_parms$formula_cand

  mm_samp <- model.matrix(fm_model, df_samp)
  mm_pop  <- model.matrix(fm_model, df_pop)
  y_samp  <- df_samp$Y
  y_pop   <- df_pop$Y                      # kept for diagnostics / A/B checks
  pi_true_pop <- sim_parms$pi_true_pop     # soft labels used for evaluation

  n_pop   <- nrow(mm_pop)
  preds   <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_pop, ncol = 0)

  binom_fam <- binomial()

  pred_pop <- function(beta) as.numeric(plogis(mm_pop %*% beta))

  add_pred <- function(preds, values, name) {
    preds <- cbind(preds, values)
    colnames(preds)[ncol(preds)] <- name
    preds
  }

  # --- need_* flags. Naming convention matches GUSTO sim:
  #   _f  = flat (unpenalised MLE)
  #   _j  = Jeffreys (Firth-penalised PE)
  #   _lf = log-F prior
  #   _br = Bayesian ridge
  # Within each family: _mk = MacKay (probit) approximation; _pp = self-
  # projection; _cpp = constrained (CITL-preserving) self-projection.
  need_mle <- any(c("pe_f", "pm_f", "pm_f_mk", "pm_f_pp", "pm_f_cpp") %in% settings$methods)
  need_j   <- any(c("pe_j", "pm_j", "pm_j_mk", "pm_j_pp", "pm_j_cpp") %in% settings$methods)
  need_lf  <- any(c("pe_lf", "pm_lf", "pm_lf_mk", "pm_lf_pp", "pm_lf_cpp") %in% settings$methods)
  need_br  <- any(c("pe_br", "pm_br", "pm_br_mk", "pm_br_pp", "pm_br_cpp") %in% settings$methods)

  # Coverage of credible intervals is requested if cri_cov is a non-empty
  # numeric vector. Any family that runs will need its posterior covariance
  # for coverage even if no PM method consumes it, so the V_* guards below
  # are widened to also fire when cri_wanted is TRUE.
  cri_wanted <- isTRUE(length(settings$cri_cov) > 0)
  eta_pseudo_pop <- if (cri_wanted) sim_parms$eta_pseudo_pop else NULL
  posteriors <- list()  # collects (mu, V) per Bayesian family for coverage

  # --- Unpenalised MLE family (flat prior) ---
  reg <- NULL
  if (need_mle || need_j) {
    reg <- tryCatch(
      glm.fit(mm_samp, y_samp, family = binom_fam),
      warning = function(w) suppressWarnings(glm.fit(mm_samp, y_samp, family = binom_fam))
    )
    if (!reg$converged) return(NULL)

    mu_f <- reg$coefficients
    V_f  <- NULL

    if (any(c("pm_f", "pm_f_mk", "pm_f_pp", "pm_f_cpp") %in% settings$methods) ||
        (cri_wanted && need_mle)) {
      w <- reg$weights
      V_f <- chol2inv(chol(crossprod(mm_samp * sqrt(w))))
    }
    if (cri_wanted && need_mle && !is.null(V_f)) {
      posteriors[["pe_f"]] <- list(mu = mu_f, V = V_f)
    }

    if ("pe_f" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(mu_f), "pe_f")
    }
    if ("pm_f" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_f, V_f), "pm_f")
    }
    if ("pm_f_mk" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_f, V_f, "mackay"), "pm_f_mk")
    }
    if (any(c("pm_f_pp", "pm_f_cpp") %in% settings$methods)) {
      pm_samp_f <- predict_pm(mm_samp, mu_f, V_f)
    }
    if ("pm_f_pp" %in% settings$methods) {
      reg_f_pp <- suppressWarnings(glm.fit(mm_samp, pm_samp_f, family = binom_fam))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(reg_f_pp$coefficients), "pm_f_pp")
    }
    if ("pm_f_cpp" %in% settings$methods) {
      cfs <- project_constrained_kl(pm_samp_f, mm_samp, sum(y_samp))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(cfs), "pm_f_cpp")
    }
  }

  # --- Jeffreys family (Firth-penalised PE) ---
  if (need_j) {
    reg_glm <- tryCatch({
      fit <- suppressWarnings(glm(fm_model, data = df_samp, family = binomial()))
      update(fit, method = "brglmFit", type = "AS_mean")
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (is.null(reg_glm) || !reg_glm$converged) return(NULL)

    mu_j <- coef(reg_glm)
    V_j  <- NULL

    if (any(c("pm_j", "pm_j_mk", "pm_j_pp", "pm_j_cpp") %in% settings$methods) ||
        cri_wanted) {
      V_j <- vcov(reg_glm)
    }
    if (cri_wanted && !is.null(V_j)) {
      posteriors[["pe_j"]] <- list(mu = mu_j, V = V_j)
    }

    if ("pe_j" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(mu_j), "pe_j")
    }
    if ("pm_j" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_j, V_j), "pm_j")
    }
    if ("pm_j_mk" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_j, V_j, "mackay"), "pm_j_mk")
    }
    if (any(c("pm_j_pp", "pm_j_cpp") %in% settings$methods)) {
      pm_samp_j <- predict_pm(mm_samp, mu_j, V_j)
    }
    if ("pm_j_pp" %in% settings$methods) {
      reg_j_pp <- suppressWarnings(glm.fit(mm_samp, pm_samp_j, family = binom_fam))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(reg_j_pp$coefficients), "pm_j_pp")
    }
    if ("pm_j_cpp" %in% settings$methods) {
      cfs <- project_constrained_kl(pm_samp_j, mm_samp, sum(y_samp))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(cfs), "pm_j_cpp")
    }
  }

  # --- log-F(m, m) family ---
  # Weakly-informative shrinkage prior on slopes (intercept flat).
  # See Greenland & Mansournia 2015. logf_glm() is in core.R.
  if (need_lf) {
    reg_lf <- tryCatch(
      logf_glm(mm_samp, y_samp, m = settings$logf_m),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(reg_lf) || !reg_lf$converged) return(NULL)

    mu_lf <- coef(reg_lf)
    V_lf  <- NULL

    if (any(c("pm_lf", "pm_lf_mk", "pm_lf_pp", "pm_lf_cpp") %in% settings$methods) ||
        cri_wanted) {
      V_lf <- vcov(reg_lf)
    }
    if (cri_wanted && !is.null(V_lf)) {
      posteriors[["pe_lf"]] <- list(mu = mu_lf, V = V_lf)
    }

    if ("pe_lf" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(mu_lf), "pe_lf")
    }
    if ("pm_lf" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_lf, V_lf), "pm_lf")
    }
    if ("pm_lf_mk" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, mu_lf, V_lf, "mackay"), "pm_lf_mk")
    }
    if (any(c("pm_lf_pp", "pm_lf_cpp") %in% settings$methods)) {
      pm_samp_lf <- predict_pm(mm_samp, mu_lf, V_lf)
    }
    if ("pm_lf_pp" %in% settings$methods) {
      reg_lf_pp <- suppressWarnings(glm.fit(mm_samp, pm_samp_lf, family = binom_fam))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(reg_lf_pp$coefficients), "pm_lf_pp")
    }
    if ("pm_lf_cpp" %in% settings$methods) {
      cfs <- project_constrained_kl(pm_samp_lf, mm_samp, sum(y_samp))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(cfs), "pm_lf_cpp")
    }
  }

  # --- Bayesian ridge family ---
  if (need_br) {
    reg_br <- tryCatch(
      bayesian_ridge(mm_samp, y_samp),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(reg_br)) return(NULL)

    if (cri_wanted) {
      posteriors[["pe_br"]] <- list(mu = reg_br$beta, V = reg_br$V)
    }

    if ("pe_br" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(reg_br$beta), "pe_br")
    }
    if ("pm_br" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, reg_br$beta, reg_br$V), "pm_br")
    }
    if ("pm_br_mk" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, predict_pm(mm_pop, reg_br$beta, reg_br$V, "mackay"), "pm_br_mk")
    }
    if (any(c("pm_br_pp", "pm_br_cpp") %in% settings$methods)) {
      pm_samp_br <- predict_pm(mm_samp, reg_br$beta, reg_br$V)
    }
    if ("pm_br_pp" %in% settings$methods) {
      reg_br_pp <- suppressWarnings(glm.fit(mm_samp, pm_samp_br, family = binom_fam))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(reg_br_pp$coefficients), "pm_br_pp")
    }
    if ("pm_br_cpp" %in% settings$methods) {
      cfs <- project_constrained_kl(pm_samp_br, mm_samp, sum(y_samp))
      preds <- add_pred(preds, pred_pop(cfs), "pm_br_cpp")
    }
  }


  # --- LASSO ---
  if (any(c("pe_lasso_min", "pe_lasso_1se") %in% settings$methods)) {
    mm_samp_noint <- mm_samp[, -1, drop = FALSE]
    mm_pop_noint  <- mm_pop[, -1, drop = FALSE]

    reg_s <- tryCatch(
      cv.glmnet(mm_samp_noint, y_samp, family = "binomial", type.measure = "deviance"),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(reg_s)) return(NULL)

    if ("pe_lasso_min" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, as.numeric(
        predict(reg_s, newx = mm_pop_noint, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
      ), "pe_lasso_min")
    }
    if ("pe_lasso_1se" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, as.numeric(
        predict(reg_s, newx = mm_pop_noint, s = "lambda.1se", type = "response")
      ), "pe_lasso_1se")
    }
  }

  # --- Ridge ---
  if (any(c("pe_ridge_min", "pe_ridge_1se") %in% settings$methods)) {
    mm_samp_noint <- mm_samp[, -1, drop = FALSE]
    mm_pop_noint  <- mm_pop[, -1, drop = FALSE]

    reg_s <- tryCatch(
      cv.glmnet(mm_samp_noint, y_samp, alpha = 0, family = "binomial", type.measure = "deviance"),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(reg_s)) return(NULL)

    if ("pe_ridge_min" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, as.numeric(
        predict(reg_s, newx = mm_pop_noint, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
      ), "pe_ridge_min")
    }
    if ("pe_ridge_1se" %in% settings$methods) {
      preds <- add_pred(preds, as.numeric(
        predict(reg_s, newx = mm_pop_noint, s = "lambda.1se", type = "response")
      ), "pe_ridge_1se")
    }
  }

  # --- Metrics ---
  # All metrics evaluated against pi_true_pop (soft labels), not y_pop.
  if (ncol(preds) == 0) return(NULL)

  method_names <- colnames(preds)
  n_methods <- length(method_names)
  # Preallocate room for: standard metrics + NB threshold variants + per
  # Bayesian family per replicate: cri_cov levels + 3 PIT-quantile scalars
  # (pit_F_10, pit_F_50, pit_F_90).
  n_cov_rows <- if (cri_wanted) length(posteriors) * length(settings$cri_cov) else 0L
  n_pit_rows <- if (cri_wanted) length(posteriors) * 3L                         else 0L
  iter_res <- vector("list",
                     length(settings$metrics) + length(settings$z_quant) +
                       n_cov_rows + n_pit_rows)
  k <- 0L
  
   if ("dkl" %in% settings$metrics) {
    k <- k + 1L
    eps <- 1e-15
    preds_c <- pmin(pmax(preds, eps), 1 - eps)
    pi_c    <- pmin(pmax(pi_true_pop, eps), 1 - eps)
  
    # Broadcast: preds_c is n × n_methods, pi_c is length n.
    # log2(pi_c / preds_c) gets pi_c recycled column-wise correctly when
    # preds_c is a matrix.
    log_term_1 <- log2(pi_c / preds_c)        # n × n_methods
    log_term_0 <- log2((1 - pi_c) / (1 - preds_c))
    kl_mat <- pi_c * log_term_1 + (1 - pi_c) * log_term_0
    iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
      metric = "dkl", method = method_names,
      value  = colMeans(kl_mat)
    )
  }
  
  
  # MSE
  if ("mse" %in% settings$metrics) {
    k <- k + 1L
    iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
      metric = "mse", method = method_names,
      value = colMeans(pi_true_pop*(preds - 1)^2+(1-pi_true_pop)*(preds - 0)^2)
    )
  }

  # Weighted c-statistic with soft labels
  if ("cstat" %in% settings$metrics) {
    k <- k + 1L
    values <- numeric(n_methods)
    for (j in seq_len(n_methods)) {
      values[j] <- cstat_soft(preds[, j], pi_true_pop)
    }
    iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
      metric = "cstat", method = method_names, value = values
    )
  }

  # O/E ratio: observed (expected under true model) over expected (predicted)
  if ("oe_ratio" %in% settings$metrics) {
    k <- k + 1L
    obs <- sum(pi_true_pop)
    iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
      metric = "oe_ratio", method = method_names,
      value = obs / colSums(preds)
    )
  }

  # Calibration intercept & slope: logistic recalibration with pi_true
  # as the "outcome" on the response side.
  #
  # Failure handling: when predictions are near-constant on the logit
  # scale (typical for heavily-shrunk LASSO/ridge at low EPV), glm.fit
  # can fail to converge and return NA coefficients. We replace these
  # with the null-model equivalents:
  #   - cal_slp NA -> 0 (no information in predictions beyond intercept)
  #   - cal_int NA -> logit(mean pi_true) - logit(mean pred)
  #     (offset between mean true and mean predicted on logit scale)
  want_cal_int <- "cal_int" %in% settings$metrics
  want_cal_slp <- "cal_slp" %in% settings$metrics

  if (want_cal_int || want_cal_slp) {
    cal_int_vals <- numeric(n_methods)
    cal_slp_vals <- numeric(n_methods)
    mean_pi_true <- mean(pi_true_pop)

    for (j in seq_len(n_methods)) {
      logit_p <- qlogis(pmin(pmax(preds[, j], 1e-15), 1 - 1e-15))
      fit_cal <- suppressWarnings(
        glm.fit(cbind(1, logit_p), pi_true_pop, family = binom_fam)
      )
      ci <- fit_cal$coefficients[1]
      cs <- fit_cal$coefficients[2]

      # If the fit failed (non-convergence or NA coefficients), fall back
      # to the null-model calibration values.
      if (!isTRUE(fit_cal$converged) || is.na(cs) || is.na(ci)) {
        cs <- 0
        mean_pred <- mean(pmin(pmax(preds[, j], 1e-15), 1 - 1e-15))
        ci <- qlogis(mean_pi_true) - qlogis(mean_pred)
      }

      cal_int_vals[j] <- ci
      cal_slp_vals[j] <- cs
    }

    if (want_cal_int) {
      k <- k + 1L
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
        metric = "cal_int", method = method_names, value = cal_int_vals
      )
    }
    if (want_cal_slp) {
      k <- k + 1L
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
        metric = "cal_slp", method = method_names, value = cal_slp_vals
      )
    }
  }

  # Net benefit with soft labels:
  # NB(z) = mean over pop of { I(pi_hat > z) * [pi_true - (1 - pi_true) * z/(1-z)] }
  #       - max(0, mean over pop of [pi_true - (1 - pi_true) * z/(1-z)])
  # Standardised net benefit with soft labels. 
  # prevalence; sNB = NB / prev, which puts results from different prevalences
  # on a comparable scale (Riley convention). Per-replicate sNB is labelled
  # snb<zm> (e.g., "snb0.5") so it can be pooled across scenarios.
  if ("snb" %in% settings$metrics) {
    scen_prev <- sim_parms$design_row$prevalence
    for (zm in settings$z_quant) {
      z <-  quantile(pi_true_pop, zm)# zm * scen_prev TODO
      k <- k + 1L
      snb_term <- pi_true_pop - (1 - pi_true_pop) * z / (1 - z)
      snb0 <- max(0, mean(snb_term))
      values <- numeric(n_methods)
      for (j in seq_len(n_methods)) {
        values[j] <- (mean((preds[, j] >= z) * snb_term) - snb0) / scen_prev
      }
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
        metric = paste0("snb", zm), method = method_names, value = values
      )
    }
  }

  # --- Posterior-distribution calibration ---
  # For each Bayesian family with a stashed posterior, compute:
  #   (a) Coverage of the eta-scale credible interval at each cri_cov level.
  #       Does the interval cover eta_pseudo_pop on average?
  #   (b) Three PIT-quantile checks summarising whether the full posterior
  #       distribution is calibrated (not just at the 95% interval). Per
  #       individual, define u_i = Phi((eta_pseudo_i - mu_i) / sigma_i).
  #       Under perfect calibration the u_i are uniformly distributed on
  #       [0,1]. Three checks:
  #         pit_F_10 : F_emp(0.10);  target = 0.10  (lower-tail mass)
  #         pit_F_50 : F_emp(0.50);  target = 0.50  (median location)
  #         pit_F_90 : F_emp(0.90);  target = 0.90  (upper-tail mass)
  # The pattern across the three is interpretable: both 10% and 90% above
  # target = posterior too narrow; both below target = too wide; both
  # shifted in the same direction = location bias.
  # All metrics are properties of the prior + Laplace approximation, not
  # of any deployment computation. Method label is the family's PE key.
  #
  # Reference: the candidate's pseudo-true linear predictor (eta_pseudo_pop),
  # computed once per scenario in generate_from_design(). When the candidate
  # nests the truth (p_cand >= p_true), this equals the true eta. Under
  # underspecification (p_cand < p_true), it is the KL projection target.
  if (cri_wanted && length(posteriors) > 0) {
    for (fam_key in names(posteriors)) {
      post <- posteriors[[fam_key]]
      mu_pop  <- as.numeric(mm_pop %*% post$mu)
      var_pop <- rowSums(mm_pop * (mm_pop %*% post$V))
      # Defensive: numerical noise can produce tiny negatives.
      sd_pop  <- sqrt(pmax(var_pop, 0))

      # (a) CrI coverage at each requested level.
      for (lvl in settings$cri_cov) {
        z   <- stats::qnorm(1 - (1 - lvl) / 2)
        lo  <- mu_pop - z * sd_pop
        hi  <- mu_pop + z * sd_pop
        cov <- mean(eta_pseudo_pop >= lo & eta_pseudo_pop <= hi)
        k <- k + 1L
        iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(
          metric = sprintf("cri_cov_%d", round(100 * lvl)),
          method = fam_key,
          value  = cov
        )
      }

      # (b) PIT-quantile checks. Defensive zero-sd guard: individuals with
      # sd_pop = 0 (rare; only when X row is in the null space of V) are
      # dropped from the PIT vector.
      ok        <- sd_pop > 0
      pit       <- pnorm((eta_pseudo_pop[ok] - mu_pop[ok]) / sd_pop[ok])
      n_pit     <- length(pit)
      if (n_pit > 0) {
        pit_F_10 <- mean(pit <= 0.10)   # target 0.10
        pit_F_50 <- mean(pit <= 0.50)   # target 0.50
        pit_F_90 <- mean(pit <= 0.90)   # target 0.90
      } else {
        pit_F_10 <- pit_F_50 <- pit_F_90 <- NA_real_
      }
      k <- k + 1L
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(metric = "pit_F_10", method = fam_key, value = pit_F_10)
      k <- k + 1L
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(metric = "pit_F_50", method = fam_key, value = pit_F_50)
      k <- k + 1L
      iter_res[[k]] <- data.frame(metric = "pit_F_90", method = fam_key, value = pit_F_90)
    }
  }

  out <- do.call(rbind, iter_res[seq_len(k)])
  r <- sim_parms$design_row
  out$row_idx       <- i_row
  out$scenario      <- r$scenario
  out$iteration     <- r$iteration
  out$true_p        <- r$true_p
  out$candidate_p   <- r$candidate_p
  out$prevalence    <- r$prevalence
  out$epv           <- r$epv
  out$n_sample      <- r$n_sample
  out$n_binary      <- r$n_binary
  out$correlation   <- r$correlation
  out$realized_prev <- mean(y_samp)
  out$n_events      <- sum(y_samp)

  rownames(out) <- NULL
  out
}
```




```{R}
# =========================================================================
# Main execution
# =========================================================================
df_design <- generate_sim_design(settings)

# --- Test mode: sample a few rows to verify pipeline ---
test_mode <- FALSE
n_test <- 10

if (test_mode) {
  test_rows <- sample(nrow(df_design), n_test)
  df_design <- df_design[test_rows, ]
  message(sprintf("TEST MODE: running %d random rows", n_test))
}

n_rows <- nrow(df_design)

chunks <- split(seq_len(n_rows), ceiling(seq_len(n_rows) / settings$chunk_size))
n_chunks <- length(chunks)

no_cores <- if (is.null(settings$n_cores)) {
  min(parallel::detectCores() - 1, n_chunks)
} else {
  settings$n_cores
}

if (no_cores > 1) {
  cl <- makeCluster(no_cores)
  registerDoSNOW(cl)
  parallel::clusterExport(cl, varlist = c(
    "df_design", "settings", "chunks",
    "sim", "generate_from_design", "predict_pm"
  ))
  # Workers also need cstat_soft and any other helpers from core.R
  parallel::clusterCall(cl, function() source("core.R"))
  message(paste("Running in PARALLEL on", no_cores, "cores,", n_chunks, "chunks"))
} else {
  registerDoSEQ()
  cl <- NULL
  message(paste("Running SEQUENTIALLY,", n_chunks, "chunks"))
}

pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_chunks, style = 3)
progress <- function(n) {setTxtProgressBar(pb, n); flush.console()}
opts <- if (interactive()) list(progress = progress) else list()

res <- foreach(
  ch = seq_len(n_chunks),
  .combine = "rbind",
  .options.snow = opts,
  .packages = c("glmnet", "pROC", "brglm2", "randtoolbox"),
  .errorhandling = "pass"
) %dorng% {
  rows <- chunks[[ch]]
  chunk_res <- vector("list", length(rows))
  for (k in seq_along(rows)) {
    chunk_res[[k]] <- tryCatch(
      sim(rows[k]),
      error = function(e) {
        data.frame(
          metric = NA, method = NA, value = NA,
          row_idx = rows[k], error_msg = conditionMessage(e),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    )
  }
  do.call(rbind, chunk_res)
}

close(pb)
if (!is.null(cl)) stopCluster(cl)

# --- Post-processing ---


is_error <- is.na(res$metric)
if (any(is_error)) {
  errors <- res[is_error, ]
  res <- res[!is_error, ]

  n_failed <- length(unique(errors$row_idx))
  message(sprintf("%d / %d rows failed (%.1f%%).", n_failed, n_rows, 100 * n_failed / n_rows))

  failed_design <- df_design[unique(errors$row_idx), ]
  cat("\nFailure breakdown:\n")
  print(table(true_p = failed_design$true_p, candidate_p = failed_design$candidate_p, epv = failed_design$epv))

  saveRDS(errors,
          file = file.path(res_dir, paste0("sim_errors.rds")))
} else {
  message("All rows completed successfully.")
}

if ("error_msg" %in% names(res)) res$error_msg <- NULL
res$metric <- as.factor(res$metric)
res$method <- as.factor(res$method)

# Count extreme calibration outliers by method (|value| > 3 on either
# cal_int or cal_slp). These are typically driven by LASSO / ridge
# collapsing to a near-constant prediction, which makes the recalibration
# regression ill-conditioned. Raw values are preserved; plotting layer
# winsorizes at ±3 for display.
cal_outliers <- res %>%
  dplyr::filter(as.character(metric) %in% c("cal_int", "cal_slp")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(is_outlier = abs(value) > 3) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method, metric) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total   = dplyr::n(),
    n_outlier = sum(is_outlier, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct       = 100 * n_outlier / n_total,
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_outlier > 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(pct))

if (nrow(cal_outliers) > 0) {
  cat("\nExtreme calibration values (|value| > 3):\n")
  print(as.data.frame(cal_outliers), row.names = FALSE)
} else {
  message("No extreme calibration values detected.")
}

saveRDS(res, file = file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_results.rds"))

message(sprintf(
  "Done. %d result rows from %d successful iterations. Saved to %s/sim_res.rds",
  nrow(res), length(unique(res$row_idx)), res_dir
))
```
















## Plots and tables

```{r}

res <- readRDS(paste0(res_dir, "sim_synth_results.rds"))

df_pmpeeff <- data.frame(z=double(), epv=integer(), method=character(), nb=double())
for(z_ in settings$z_quant){
  for(method_ in c("pe","pm")){
    for(epv_ in c(5,10)){
      A <- mean((res |> filter(epv==epv_ & method %in% paste0(method_, c("_f","_j","_lf","_br")) & metric==paste0("snb",z_)) |> select(value))[[1]])
      df_pmpeeff <- rbind(df_pmpeeff, data.frame(z=z_, epv=epv_, method=method_, nb=A))
    }
  }
}

nms <- apply(df_pmpeeff, 1, function(x){sprintf("%s|%s|%s", x['z'],x['epv'],x['method'])})
rownames(df_pmpeeff) <- gsub(" ", "", nms)

saveRDS(df_pmpeeff, file.path(res_dir,"synth_pmpeeff.rds"))
# =========================================================================
# Main-effects grid plots
#   Rows = metrics, cols = factor levels, x = EPV
#   Point + whiskers, no connecting lines, methods dodged horizontally
#   Three separate figures, one per column-facet factor:
#     Figure A: cols = true_p levels
#     Figure B: cols = candidate_p levels
#     Figure C: cols = prevalence levels
#   Aggregation: mean (median for cal_slp and oe_ratio, which have
#   heavy-tailed distributions) as the centrality estimate. Whiskers
#   always show the IQR (Q25, Q75) across replicates — chosen over SD
#   so that whiskers stay within the metric's natural range (e.g., MSE
#   stays non-negative) and remain robust to outlier replicates.
# =========================================================================


# =========================================================================
# Method registry — single source of truth for plotting.
#
# Each method gets one row. The four lookup vectors used by the plot
# builders (method_labels, method_colors, method_shapes, default order)
# are derived from this. Adding a new method to the plots = adding a row.
#
# Naming convention (documentation only; not enforced):
#   <estimator>_<prior>[_<computation>]
#     estimator : pe (PE / point estimate) or pm (PM / posterior mean)
#     prior     : f (flat), j (Jeffreys/Firth), lf (log-F), br (Bayesian
#                 ridge), ridge / lasso (CV-tuned)
#     comp.     : omitted = quadrature (exact); mk = MacKay;
#                 pp = self-projection; cpp = constrained (CITL-preserving)
#                 self-projection; min / 1se for CV-tuned LASSO/ridge
#
# Non-quadrature PM rows (_mk / _pp / _cpp) are commented out by default
# so the main plot stays uncluttered. They are still produced by sim()
# and consumed by build_computation_plot(). Uncomment any row to also
# include it in the main plot.
# =========================================================================

q_range <- c(0.25, 0.75)

method_registry <- tibble::tribble(
  ~key,            ~label,                                 ~color,     ~shape,
  "pe_f",          "PE (flat)",                           "#1F4E79",  1,
  "pm_f",          "PM (flat)",                           "#1F4E79",  16,
  "pm_f_mk",       "PM (flat, MacKay)",                   "#1F4E79",  16,
  "pm_f_pp",       "PM (flat, self-proj.)",               "#1F4E79",  16,
  "pm_f_cpp",      "PM (flat, constr. self-proj.)",       "#1F4E79",  16,

  "pe_j",          "PE (Jeffreys)",                       "#884553",  0,
  "pm_j",          "PM (Jeffreys)",                       "#884553",  15,
  "pm_j_mk",       "PM (Jeffreys, MacKay)",               "#884553",  15,
  "pm_j_pp",       "PM (Jeffreys, self-proj.)",           "#884553",  15,
  "pm_j_cpp",      "PM (Jeffreys, constr. self-proj.)",   "#884553",  15,

  "pe_lf",         "PE (log-F)",                          "#009E73",  2,
  "pm_lf",         "PM (logF)",                           "#009E73",  17,
  "pm_lf_mk",      "PM (logF, MacKay)",                   "#009E73",  17,
  "pm_lf_pp",      "PM (logF, self-proj.)",               "#009E73",  17,
  "pm_lf_cpp",     "PM (logF, constr. self-proj.)",       "#009E73",  17,

  "pe_br",         "PE (Bayes. ridge)",                       "#E07B00",  5,
  "pm_br",         "PM (Bayes. ridge)",                       "#E07B00",  18,
  "pm_br_mk",      "PM (Bayes. ridge, MacKay)",               "#E07B00",  18,
  "pm_br_pp",      "PM (Bayes. ridge, self-proj.)",           "#E07B00",  18,
  "pm_br_cpp",     "PM (Bayes. ridge, constr. self-proj.)",   "#E07B00",  18,

  "pe_ridge_min",  "Ridge (CV-min)",                       "#AAAAAA",  2,
  "pe_lasso_min",  "LASSO (CV-min)",                       "#AAAAAA",  6,
)

# ---- Derive lookup vectors ----
method_labels <- setNames(method_registry$label, method_registry$key)
method_colors <- setNames(method_registry$color, method_registry$key)
method_shapes <- setNames(method_registry$shape, method_registry$key)
default_method_order <- method_registry$key

# all_metrics[grep("^snb", all_metrics)]
# snb_quants <- sub("^snb", "", all_metrics[grepl("^snb", all_metrics)])

metric_labels <- c(
  dkl      = "KL divergence \n (bits)",
  mse      = "MSE",
  cstat    = "C-statistic",
  cal_int  = "Calibration intercept",
  cal_slp  = "Calibration slope",
  oe_ratio = "O/E ratio",
  snb0.25   = "sNB (z = 25th \n percentile of true risks)",
  snb0.5    = "sNB (z = median of \n true risks)",
  snb0.75   = "sNB (z = 75th \n percentile of true risk)"
)

# Metrics shown as rows (default). oe_ratio available but off by default;
# add it by passing metric_order = c(..., "oe_ratio") to build_plot().
default_metric_order <- c("dkl", "mse", "cstat", "oe_ratio", "cal_slp", "snb0.25", "snb0.5", "snb0.75")

metric_reference <- list(
  cal_int  = 0,
  cal_slp  = 1,
  oe_ratio = 1
)

# ---- Per-metric summary specification ----
# For each metric, independently choose:
#   - `center`: which centrality to display ("mean" or "median")
#   - `range` : how to compute lower/upper whiskers ("sd" or "quant")
# When range = "quant", the quantile pair comes from the global q_range
# vector defined above (default: c(0.25, 0.75)).
# To override for a particular plot, pass a modified list to the
# `summary_spec` argument of build_plot()/build_marginal_plot()/etc.
metric_summary_spec <- list(
  dkl      = list(center = "median", range = "quant"), 
  mse      = list(center = "mean",   range = "sd"),
  cstat    = list(center = "mean",   range = "sd"),
  cal_int  = list(center = "median",   range = "quant"),
  cal_slp  = list(center = "median", range = "quant"),
  oe_ratio = list(center = "median", range = "quant"),
  snb0.25  = list(center = "mean",   range = "sd"),
  snb0.5   = list(center = "mean",   range = "sd"),
  snb0.75  = list(center = "mean",   range = "sd")
)

# ---- Centrality / whisker helpers ----
# Each helper takes the per-metric data vector x and a single-string
# spec, returning a scalar. compute_lower / compute_upper are split
# (rather than one helper returning length-2) so that summarise() can
# call them directly without list-unpacking.

compute_center <- function(x, spec) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) < 1) return(NA_real_)
  switch(spec,
         mean   = mean(x),
         median = median(x),
         stop("Unknown centrality spec: ", spec))
}

compute_lower <- function(x, spec) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  switch(spec,
         sd    = mean(x) - sd(x),
         quant = unname(quantile(x, q_range[1], na.rm = TRUE)),
         stop("Unknown range spec: ", spec))
}

compute_upper <- function(x, spec) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  switch(spec,
         sd    = mean(x) + sd(x),
         quant = unname(quantile(x, q_range[2], na.rm = TRUE)),
         stop("Unknown range spec: ", spec))
}

# Resolve the metric's summary spec at aggregation time. `metric_key`
# is the lowercase key (e.g., "mse"), not the display label. Returns
# the list (center, range) for that metric, falling back to mean+quant
# if the metric isn't in the spec.
get_summary_spec <- function(metric_key, spec_table = metric_summary_spec) {
  s <- spec_table[[metric_key]]
  if (is.null(s)) return(list(center = "mean", range = "quant"))
  s
}

# ---- Prepare data ----
# Keep metric as character here; build_plot() subsets and levels it based
# on the metric_order argument supplied at call time.
res_plot <- res %>%
  mutate(
    method = as.character(method),
    metric = as.character(metric)
  ) %>%
  mutate(metric = ifelse(metric %in% names(metric_labels),
                         metric_labels[metric], metric))

# ---- Plot builder ----
# Each panel has its own y-axis (free in both directions): within-panel
# method comparison is the only thing that matters. Point-and-whisker
# display, dodged by method.
# method_order: character vector of method keys in the order to display.
#   Controls both legend order and dodge order. Unknown keys are dropped;
#   data methods not in the order are appended at the end.
build_plot <- function(data, col_factor, col_label_fn, figure_title,
                       method_order  = default_method_order,
                       metric_order  = default_metric_order,
                       summary_spec  = metric_summary_spec) {

  # Validate & finalise method ordering. Keep only methods that (a) are
  # in the data, (b) are known to method_labels/colors/shapes, AND (c)
  # are either point estimates or quadrature PMs. Non-quadrature PM
  # variants (_mk / _pp / _cpp) are filtered out — they belong on the
  # dedicated computation-comparison plot. This keeps the main figure
  # focused on prior-family comparison.
  is_main_plot_method <- function(k) {
    parts <- strsplit(k, "_", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (length(parts) < 1) return(FALSE)
    if (parts[1] == "pe") return(TRUE)
    if (parts[1] == "pm") return(length(parts) <= 2)
    TRUE
  }
  avail <- unique(as.character(data$method))
  known <- names(method_labels)
  candidates <- intersect(avail, known)
  candidates <- candidates[vapply(candidates, is_main_plot_method, logical(1))]
  method_order <- method_order[method_order %in% candidates]
  extra <- setdiff(candidates, method_order)
  method_order <- c(method_order, extra)

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% method_order) %>%
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = method_order))

  # Subset to requested metrics and re-level in requested order
  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(metric) %in% metric_labels[metric_order]) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(as.character(metric),
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order]))

  # Per-metric aggregation. The summary spec for each metric (centrality
  # and range type) is looked up via metric_summary_spec; see helpers
  # compute_center / compute_lower / compute_upper.
  data$metric_key <- names(metric_labels)[match(as.character(data$metric),
                                                metric_labels)]

  agg <- data %>%
    group_by(metric, metric_key, method, !!sym(col_factor), epv) %>%
    summarise(
      n_scen = sum(!is.na(value)),
      center = compute_center(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$center),
      lo     = compute_lower(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      hi     = compute_upper(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(-metric_key) %>%
    rename(mean = center)  # keep `mean` name so ggplot aes doesn't change

  dodge_width <- 0.6

  # Reference-line data frame, keyed by metric factor
  ref_df <- data.frame(
    metric_key = names(metric_reference),
    yint       = unlist(metric_reference)
  ) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric_labels[metric_key],
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order])) %>%
    filter(!is.na(metric)) %>%
    select(metric, yint)

  p <- ggplot(agg, aes(x = factor(epv), y = mean,
                       color = method, shape = method, group = method)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                  width = 0.3,
                  position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
                  linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8,
               position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggh4x::facet_grid2(
      rows = vars(metric),
      cols = vars(!!sym(col_factor)),
      labeller = labeller(
        metric = label_value,
        .cols = col_label_fn
      ),
      scales = "free_y",
      independent = "y"   # every panel gets its own y-scale
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels[method_order],
                       breaks = method_order, drop = FALSE, name = "Method") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = method_shapes, labels = method_labels[method_order],
                       breaks = method_order, drop = FALSE, name = "Method") +
    labs(
      title    = NULL,
      subtitle = NULL,
      x = "Events per variable (EPV)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
      strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 9),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9)
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE),
           shape = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

  if (nrow(ref_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(
      data = ref_df,
      aes(yintercept = yint),
      linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    )
  }

  p
}

# ---- Column label formatters ----
label_true_p      <- function(x) paste0("True p = ", x)
label_candidate_p <- function(x) paste0("Candidate p = ", x)
label_prevalence  <- function(x) paste0("Prev = ",
                                        scales::percent(as.numeric(as.character(x)),
                                                        accuracy = 1))

# ---- Build three figures ----
plots <- list()

plots$true_p <- build_plot(
  res_plot, "true_p", label_true_p,
  "Performance by size of true model"
)

plots$candidate_p <- build_plot(
  res_plot, "candidate_p", label_candidate_p,
  "Performance by size of candidate model"
)

plots$prevalence <- build_plot(
  res_plot, "prevalence", label_prevalence,
  "Performance by outcome prevalence"
)

# ---- Save (taller than wide for 7×3 grid) ----
fig_width  <- 9
fig_height <- 14

ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_by_true_p.png"),
       plots$true_p, width = fig_width, height = fig_height, dpi = 200)
ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_by_candidate_p.png"),
       plots$candidate_p, width = fig_width, height = fig_height, dpi = 200)
ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_by_prevalence.png"),
       plots$prevalence, width = fig_width, height = fig_height, dpi = 200)

message("Saved 3 figures to ", res_dir, "/")

# =========================================================================
# Marginal-effects figure
#   Rows = metrics, columns = the four main design factors (EPV, prev,
#   true_p, candidate_p). Within each panel, each method is shown as
#   point + whiskers at each level of the column's factor, marginalising
#   over all other factors (including randomized correlation and n_binary).
#   This complements the by-factor figures by presenting one-way marginals
#   side-by-side rather than full two-way grids.
# =========================================================================

build_marginal_plot <- function(data, figure_title,
                                 col_factors    = c("epv", "prevalence",
                                                    "true_p", "candidate_p"),
                                 col_titles     = c(epv         = "EPV",
                                                    prevalence  = "Prevalence",
                                                    true_p      = "True p",
                                                    candidate_p = "Candidate p"),
                                 method_order   = default_method_order,
                                 metric_order   = default_metric_order,
                                 summary_spec   = metric_summary_spec) {

  # --- Data prep, same as build_plot ---
  # Auto-filter: drop non-quadrature PM variants. They belong on the
  # computation-comparison plot.
  is_main_plot_method <- function(k) {
    parts <- strsplit(k, "_", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (length(parts) < 1) return(FALSE)
    if (parts[1] == "pe") return(TRUE)
    if (parts[1] == "pm") return(length(parts) <= 2)
    TRUE
  }
  avail <- unique(as.character(data$method))
  known <- names(method_labels)
  candidates <- intersect(avail, known)
  candidates <- candidates[vapply(candidates, is_main_plot_method, logical(1))]
  method_order <- method_order[method_order %in% candidates]
  extra <- setdiff(candidates, method_order)
  method_order <- c(method_order, extra)
  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% method_order) %>%
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = method_order))

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(metric) %in% metric_labels[metric_order]) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(as.character(metric),
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order]))

  data$metric_key <- names(metric_labels)[match(as.character(data$metric),
                                                metric_labels)]

  # Per-factor aggregation. For each col_factor, group by (metric, method,
  # that factor) and compute the appropriate center + whiskers via the
  # per-metric summary spec. Stack across factors with a `col_factor`
  # label so we can facet by it.
  agg_one <- function(col_var) {
    data %>%
      group_by(metric, metric_key, method, !!sym(col_var)) %>%
      summarise(
        center = compute_center(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$center),
        lo     = compute_lower(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
        hi     = compute_upper(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      select(-metric_key) %>%
      rename(x_level = !!sym(col_var)) %>%
      mutate(col_factor = col_var)
  }

  agg <- bind_rows(lapply(col_factors, agg_one))

  # Factor the col_factor column with pretty labels in the requested order
  agg$col_factor <- factor(agg$col_factor,
                           levels = col_factors,
                           labels = unname(col_titles[col_factors]))

  # x_level is treated as a factor within each column (different factors
  # have different levels and display correctly if each col_factor has its
  # own x scale; ggh4x independent x does exactly this).
  agg$x_level <- factor(agg$x_level)

  # Reference lines as before
  ref_df <- data.frame(
    metric_key = names(metric_reference),
    yint       = unlist(metric_reference)
  ) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric_labels[metric_key],
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order])) %>%
    filter(!is.na(metric)) %>%
    select(metric, yint)

  dodge_width <- 0.8

  p <- ggplot(agg, aes(x = x_level, y = center,
                       color = method, shape = method, group = method)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                  width = 0.3,
                  position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
                  linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 1.5,
               position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    facet_grid(
      rows = vars(metric),
      cols = vars(col_factor),
      scales = "free",
      switch = "x"  # 1. Moves the column (col_factor) labels to the bottom
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = method_colors, labels = method_labels[method_order],
                       breaks = method_order, drop = FALSE, name = "Method") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = method_shapes, labels = method_labels[method_order],
                       breaks = method_order, drop = FALSE, name = "Method") +
    labs(
      title    = NULL,
      subtitle = NULL,
      x = NULL,
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
      strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0),
      strip.placement    = "outside", # 2. Places bottom labels cleanly beneath the X-axis text
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 9),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9)
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE),
           shape = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

  if (nrow(ref_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(
      data = ref_df,
      aes(yintercept = yint),
      linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    )
  }

  p
}

plots$marginal <- build_marginal_plot(
  res_plot,
  NULL
)

ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_main.png"),
       plots$marginal, width = 12, height = 14, dpi = 200)

message("Saved marginal figure to ", res_dir, "/sim_synth_main.png")

invisible(plots)





# =========================================================================
# PM computation-method comparison plot
#
# Holds the prior fixed within each column (Flat / Jeffreys / log-F /
# Bayesian ridge) and varies the PM computation method (quadrature /
# MacKay / self-projection / CITL-preserving self-projection) within
# each panel. Aggregates across design factors (true_p, candidate_p,
# prevalence): the message of this plot is "computation choice barely
# matters relative to prior choice", and that's clearest when not
# stratified by design factor. Y-axis is matched to the main plots'
# per-metric range.
#
# Structurally analogous to GUSTO's gusto_pm_comp.png:
#   rows = metrics, cols = prior family, x = EPV, dodged by computation.
# =========================================================================

computation_labels <- c(
  exact = "Quadrature (exact)",
  mk    = "MacKay",
  pp    = "Self-projection",
  cpp   = "CITL-preserving proj."
)
computation_colors <- c(
  exact = "#1F4E79",
  mk    = "#2E75B6",
  pp    = "#E07B00",
  cpp   = "#B22222"
)
computation_shapes <- c(
  exact = 16,
  mk    = 17,
  pp    = 15,
  cpp   = 18
)
computation_order <- c("exact", "mk", "pp") #, "cpp")

family_labels <- c(
  f  = "Flat prior",
  j  = "Jeffreys (Firth)",
  lf = "log-F(2, 2)",
  br = "Gaussian (Bayes. ridge)"
)
family_order <- c("f", "j", "lf", "br")

parse_pm_method <- function(key) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "_", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  if (length(parts) < 2 || parts[1] != "pm") {
    return(c(family = NA_character_, computation = NA_character_))
  }
  fam  <- parts[2]
  comp <- if (length(parts) == 2) "exact" else parts[3]
  c(family = fam, computation = comp)
}

# ---- Capture per-metric y-limits from the main plots' aggregation ----
# Replicates the central+whisker computation of build_plot but extracts
# per-metric (ymin, ymax) across all methods and EPV values, aggregated
# across the design factor. The comp plot will use these to anchor each
# row's y-range so the visual scale matches the main plots.
get_main_plot_ylims <- function(data,
                                method_order  = default_method_order,
                                metric_order  = default_metric_order,
                                summary_spec  = metric_summary_spec) {
  is_main <- function(k) {
    parts <- strsplit(k, "_", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (length(parts) < 1) return(FALSE)
    if (parts[1] == "pe") return(TRUE)
    if (parts[1] == "pm") return(length(parts) <= 2)
    TRUE
  }
  avail <- unique(as.character(data$method))
  known <- names(method_labels)
  candidates <- intersect(avail, known)
  candidates <- candidates[vapply(candidates, is_main, logical(1))]

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% candidates) %>%
    filter(as.character(metric) %in% metric_labels[metric_order]) %>%
    mutate(metric = as.character(metric))

  data$metric_key <- names(metric_labels)[match(data$metric, metric_labels)]

  # Aggregate by (metric, method, epv) — same grouping as build_plot but
  # without the design-factor column, so per-metric limits average over
  # all design configurations and all main-plot methods.
  agg <- data %>%
    group_by(metric, metric_key, method, epv) %>%
    summarise(
      lo = compute_lower(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      hi = compute_upper(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(-metric_key)

  agg %>%
    group_by(metric) %>%
    summarise(ymin = min(lo, na.rm = TRUE),
              ymax = max(hi, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop")
}




build_computation_plot <- function(data, figure_title = NULL,
                                   metric_order  = default_metric_order,
                                   summary_spec  = metric_summary_spec,
                                   y_limits      = NULL) {

  pm_keys <- unique(as.character(data$method))
  pm_keys <- pm_keys[grepl("^pm_", pm_keys)]
  parsed  <- t(sapply(pm_keys, parse_pm_method))
  valid   <- !is.na(parsed[, "family"]) &
             parsed[, "family"]      %in% family_order &
             parsed[, "computation"] %in% computation_order
  pm_keys <- pm_keys[valid]
  parsed  <- parsed[valid, , drop = FALSE]

  if (length(pm_keys) == 0) {
    warning("No PM methods found for computation-comparison plot")
    return(NULL)
  }

  key_to_family <- setNames(parsed[, "family"], pm_keys)
  key_to_comp   <- setNames(parsed[, "computation"], pm_keys)

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% pm_keys) %>%
    mutate(
      method      = as.character(method),
      family      = factor(unname(key_to_family[method]), levels = family_order),
      computation = factor(unname(key_to_comp[method]),   levels = computation_order)
    )

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(metric) %in% metric_labels[metric_order]) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(as.character(metric),
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order]))

  data$metric_key <- names(metric_labels)[match(as.character(data$metric),
                                                metric_labels)]

  # Aggregate over design factors (true_p, candidate_p, prevalence) —
  # group only by (metric, family, computation, epv).
  agg <- data %>%
    group_by(metric, metric_key, family, computation, epv) %>%
    summarise(
      n_scen = sum(!is.na(value)),
      center = compute_center(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$center),
      lo     = compute_lower(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      hi     = compute_upper(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$range),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(-metric_key)

  ref_df <- data.frame(
    metric_key = names(metric_reference),
    yint       = unlist(metric_reference)
  ) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric_labels[metric_key],
                           levels = metric_labels[metric_order])) %>%
    filter(!is.na(metric)) %>%
    select(metric, yint)

  # ---- Anchor points to force per-row y-limits to match main plots ----
  agg_anchors <- NULL
  if (!is.null(y_limits)) {
    families_present <- levels(droplevels(agg$family))
    epvs_present     <- unique(agg$epv)
    base_grid <- expand.grid(
      metric = unique(agg$metric),
      family = families_present,
      epv    = epvs_present,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    base_grid <- base_grid %>%
      left_join(y_limits, by = "metric") %>%
      filter(!is.na(ymin), !is.na(ymax))
    agg_anchors <- bind_rows(
      base_grid %>% mutate(center = ymin),
      base_grid %>% mutate(center = ymax)
    ) %>%
      mutate(
        lo          = center,
        hi          = center,
        computation = factor(computation_order[1], levels = computation_order),
        family      = factor(family, levels = family_order),
        metric      = factor(metric, levels = metric_labels[metric_order])
      ) %>%
      select(metric, family, computation, epv, center, lo, hi)
  }

  family_labeller <- function(x) family_labels[as.character(x)]
  dodge_width <- 0.6

  p <- ggplot(agg, aes(x = factor(epv), y = center,
                       color = computation, shape = computation,
                       group = computation)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                  width = 0.3,
                  position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
                  linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8,
               position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggh4x::facet_grid2(
      rows = vars(metric),
      cols = vars(family),
      labeller = labeller(family = family_labeller),
      scales = "free_y",
      switch = "x"  # 1. Moves the column (family) labels to the bottom
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = computation_colors,
                       labels = computation_labels[computation_order],
                       breaks = computation_order,
                       drop = FALSE, name = "Computation") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = computation_shapes,
                       labels = computation_labels[computation_order],
                       breaks = computation_order,
                       drop = FALSE, name = "Computation") +
    labs(
      title    = NULL,
      subtitle = NULL,
      x = "Events per variable (EPV)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
      strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
      strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0),
      strip.placement    = "outside", # 2. Places bottom labels cleanly beneath the X-axis numbers
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 9),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1),
           shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

  if (!is.null(agg_anchors)) {
    p <- p + geom_blank(data = agg_anchors, aes(y = center))
  }

  if (nrow(ref_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(
      data = ref_df,
      aes(yintercept = yint),
      linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    )
  }

  p
}

build_computation_plot_rel <- function(data, figure_title = NULL,
                                   metric_order  = default_metric_order,
                                   summary_spec  = metric_summary_spec,
                                   y_limits      = NULL) {

  pm_keys <- unique(as.character(data$method))
  pm_keys <- pm_keys[grepl("^pm_", pm_keys)]
  parsed  <- t(sapply(pm_keys, parse_pm_method))
  valid   <- !is.na(parsed[, "family"]) &
             parsed[, "family"]      %in% family_order &
             parsed[, "computation"] %in% computation_order
  pm_keys <- pm_keys[valid]
  parsed  <- parsed[valid, , drop = FALSE]

  if (length(pm_keys) == 0) {
    warning("No PM methods found for computation-comparison plot")
    return(NULL)
  }

  key_to_family <- setNames(parsed[, "family"], pm_keys)
  key_to_comp   <- setNames(parsed[, "computation"], pm_keys)

  # 1. Initial Prep
  data_prepared <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% pm_keys) %>%
    mutate(
      method      = as.character(method),
      family      = factor(unname(key_to_family[method]), levels = family_order),
      computation = factor(unname(key_to_comp[method]),   levels = computation_order),
      metric_key  = names(metric_labels)[match(as.character(metric), metric_labels)]
    ) %>%
    filter(metric_key %in% metric_order) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(as.character(metric), levels = metric_labels[metric_order]))

  # 2. Extract Exact values per replicate paired by iteration (and scenario factors)
  # In this sim, unique scenarios are defined by epv, true_p, candidate_p, and prevalence
  exact_vals <- data_prepared %>%
    filter(computation == "exact") %>%
    select(metric, family, epv, true_p, candidate_p, prevalence, iteration, exact_value = value)

  # 3. Calculate SD of paired differences and absolute centers
  agg <- data_prepared %>%
    left_join(exact_vals, by = c("metric", "family", "epv", "true_p", "candidate_p", "prevalence", "iteration")) %>%
    mutate(diff = value - exact_value) %>%
    group_by(metric, metric_key, family, computation, epv) %>%
    summarise(
      center  = compute_center(value, get_summary_spec(first(metric_key), summary_spec)$center),
      diff_sd = sd(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # Whiskers show SD of paired difference, centered on the method's own absolute mean
      # We skip whiskers for the 'exact' method itself as it is the reference
      lo = ifelse(computation == "exact", NA, center - diff_sd),
      hi = ifelse(computation == "exact", NA, center + diff_sd)
    )

  # 4. Anchor points to force per-row y-limits to match main plots
  agg_anchors <- NULL
  if (!is.null(y_limits)) {
    families_present <- levels(droplevels(agg$family))
    epvs_present     <- unique(agg$epv)
    base_grid <- expand.grid(
      metric = unique(agg$metric),
      family = families_present,
      epv    = epvs_present,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ) %>%
      left_join(y_limits, by = "metric")
    
    agg_anchors <- bind_rows(
      base_grid %>% mutate(center = ymin),
      base_grid %>% mutate(center = ymax)
    ) %>%
      mutate(
        lo = center, hi = center,
        computation = factor(computation_order[1], levels = computation_order),
        family      = factor(family, levels = family_order),
        metric      = factor(metric, levels = levels(agg$metric))
      )
  }

  # 5. Plotting
  family_labeller <- function(x) family_labels[as.character(x)]
  dodge_width <- 0.6

  p <- ggplot(agg, aes(x = factor(epv), y = center, color = computation, shape = computation, group = computation)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                  width = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggh4x::facet_grid2(
      rows = vars(metric), cols = vars(family),
      labeller = labeller(family = family_labeller),
      scales = "free_y"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = computation_colors, labels = computation_labels, name = "Computation") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = computation_shapes, labels = computation_labels, name = "Computation") +
    labs(x = "Events per variable (EPV)", y = NULL) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
      strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )

  if (!is.null(agg_anchors)) {
    p <- p + geom_blank(data = agg_anchors, aes(y = center))
  }

  # Reference lines for absolute values
  ref_df <- data.frame(metric_key = names(metric_reference), yint = unlist(metric_reference)) %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric_labels[metric_key], levels = levels(agg$metric))) %>%
    filter(!is.na(metric))

  if (nrow(ref_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(data = ref_df, aes(yintercept = yint), 
                        linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4)
  }

  p
}

# ---- Build the comp plot ----
main_ylims <- get_main_plot_ylims(res_plot)
# Main-plot metric column uses labels (not keys) after res_plot prep, so
# translate keys to labels for matching.
main_ylims <- main_ylims %>%
  mutate(metric = unname(metric_labels[metric]))

plots$pm_comp <- build_computation_plot(
  res_plot,
  NULL,
  y_limits = main_ylims
)

ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_pm.png"),
       plots$pm_comp, width = 12, height = 14, dpi = 200)



# =========================================================================
# CrI coverage plot — credible-interval coverage stratified by specification
# regime, intended for the appendix.
#
# Posterior calibration is summarised more comprehensively in the main-text
# table (CrI coverage + PIT-based metrics, marginalised over regime). This
# plot retains the regime stratification for readers who want the
# regime-specific story.
#
# Only the four Bayesian PE keys (pe_f / pe_j / pe_lf / pe_br) appear;
# CV-tuned LASSO/ridge are excluded (no Laplace posterior).
# =========================================================================

coverage_methods <- c("pe_f", "pe_j", "pe_lf", "pe_br")
coverage_colors  <- method_colors[coverage_methods]
coverage_shapes  <- method_shapes[coverage_methods]
coverage_labels  <- c(pe_f  = "Flat",
                      pe_j  = "Jeffreys",
                      pe_lf = "log-F",
                      pe_br = "Gaussian (Bayes. ridge)")


build_coverage_plot <- function(data,
                                col_factors    = c("true_p", "candidate_p",
                                                   "prevalence", "epv"),
                                col_factor_lbl = c(true_p      = "True predictors",
                                                   candidate_p = "Candidate predictors",
                                                   prevalence  = "Prevalence",
                                                   epv         = "EPV"),
                                figure_title   = NULL) {

  data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(method) %in% coverage_methods,
           grepl("^cri_cov_", as.character(metric))) %>%
    mutate(method = factor(as.character(method), levels = coverage_methods),
           metric = as.character(metric))

  if (nrow(data) == 0) {
    warning("build_coverage_plot: no cri_cov_* rows found; check that ",
            "settings$cri_cov was non-empty when the simulation ran.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  data <- data %>%
    mutate(spec_regime = factor(
      dplyr::case_when(
        candidate_p <  true_p ~ "Underspecified",
        candidate_p == true_p ~ "Correctly specified",
        candidate_p >  true_p ~ "Overspecified"
      ),
      levels = c("Underspecified", "Correctly specified", "Overspecified")
    ))

  # Coverage level (target value) parsed from the metric name.
  data$cov_level <- as.numeric(sub("^cri_cov_", "", data$metric)) / 100

  # Per-design-factor aggregation. spec_regime kept in the grouping so
  # the three regimes remain separated regardless of which col_factor we
  # collapse over.
  agg_one <- function(col_var) {
    data %>%
      group_by(cov_level, spec_regime, method, !!sym(col_var)) %>%
      summarise(
        n_sim  = sum(!is.na(value)),
        center = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        lo     = quantile(value, q_range[1], na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
        hi     = quantile(value, q_range[2], na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      rename(x_value_raw = !!sym(col_var)) %>%
      mutate(col_factor = col_var,
             x_value    = factor(as.character(x_value_raw),
                                 levels = as.character(sort(unique(x_value_raw))))) %>%
      select(-x_value_raw)
  }
  agg <- bind_rows(lapply(col_factors, agg_one))

  # Numeric x-axis sort across all design factors.
  agg$x_value <- factor(
    as.character(agg$x_value),
    levels = unique(as.character(agg$x_value))[
      order(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(unique(as.character(agg$x_value)))))
    ]
  )
  agg$col_factor <- factor(agg$col_factor,
                           levels = col_factors,
                           labels = unname(col_factor_lbl[col_factors]))

  # Reference line at the nominal coverage level (typically 0.95).
  ref_df <- data %>% distinct(cov_level) %>% rename(yint = cov_level)

  dodge_width <- 0.6

  ggplot(agg, aes(x = x_value, y = center,
                  color = method, shape = method, group = method)) +
    geom_hline(data = ref_df, aes(yintercept = yint),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), width = 0.3,
                  position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 2, position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width)) +
    ggh4x::facet_grid2(
      rows = vars(spec_regime),
      cols = vars(col_factor),
      scales = "free"
      # `scales = "free"` (no `independent`) gives row-shared y-scale and
      # column-shared x-scale: y-ticks only in leftmost column, x-ticks
      # only in bottom row. Same convention as build_marginal_plot.
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = coverage_colors, labels = coverage_labels,
                       breaks = coverage_methods, drop = FALSE, name = "Prior") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = coverage_shapes, labels = coverage_labels,
                       breaks = coverage_methods, drop = FALSE, name = "Prior") +
    labs(
      title    = NULL,
      subtitle = NULL,
      x = NULL,
      y = "Empirical coverage"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
      strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0, size = 8),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 9),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9)
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
           shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE))
}


# ---- Appendix figure: coverage stratified by specification regime ----
plots$coverage_by_regime <- build_coverage_plot(
  res_plot,
  figure_title = "CrI coverage by prior family and specification regime"
)
ggsave(file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_coverage_by_regime.png"),
       plots$coverage_by_regime, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 200)


# =========================================================================
# Posterior calibration table — main-text summary.
#
# 12 rows (4 priors x 3 EPV levels) x 4 metric columns:
#   - CrI coverage (target = 0.95)
#   - F_emp(0.10)  (target = 0.10)
#   - F_emp(0.50)  (target = 0.50)
#   - F_emp(0.90)  (target = 0.90)
# Saved as RDS in a tidy long format with raw mean and SD across
# replicates per (prior, EPV, metric) cell. The manuscript Rmd can
# pivot/format/style this however it likes (e.g., kable + kableExtra).
# Marginalised over specification regime, prevalence, true_p, and
# candidate_p — the regime story is in the appendix coverage figure.
# =========================================================================

posterior_table <- res_plot %>%
  filter(as.character(method) %in% coverage_methods,
         as.character(metric) %in% c("cri_cov_95", "pit_F_10", "pit_F_50", "pit_F_90")) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(as.character(method),
                         levels = coverage_methods,
                         labels = unname(coverage_labels[coverage_methods])),
         metric = factor(as.character(metric),
                         levels = c("cri_cov_95", "pit_F_10", "pit_F_50", "pit_F_90"),
                         labels = c("Coverage 95%", "PIT F(0.10)",
                                    "PIT F(0.50)", "PIT F(0.90)"))) %>%
  group_by(method, epv, metric) %>%
  summarise(mean_val = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_val   = sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE), #/sqrt(sum(!is.na(value))),
            n_rep    = sum(!is.na(value)),
            .groups  = "drop") %>%
  arrange(method, epv, metric) %>%
  rename(prior = method, EPV = epv)

saveRDS(posterior_table,
        file = file.path(res_dir, "sim_synth_posterior_table.rds"))

message("Saved coverage figure and posterior-calibration table to ", res_dir, "/")

kable(posterior_table)
```
